recaps.text: The Union - a fading empire ruled from the great city of Adua - is introduced through three men whose paths have not yet crossed. In the far North, the weary warrior Logen Ninefingers survives an ambush by the inhuman Shanka, loses his companions, and is drawn south by a mysterious summons. In Adua, the crippled Inquisitor Glokta grinds confessions out of merchants suspected of treason, following the corruption higher than he is supposed to. And the vain young officer Jezal dan Luthar trains grudgingly for the great fencing Contest under Major West, while beginning to notice West's sharp-tongued sister, Ardee.

As Glokta's investigation into the merchant guilds widens, it becomes clear that powerful men want certain questions left unasked, and his master, Arch Lector Sult, points him at ever more dangerous targets. Into the city arrives a bald old man calling himself Bayaz, First of the Magi, demanding an ancient seat on the ruling Closed Council and unsettling everyone who assumed his order was a legend. Far to the south, the escaped slave Ferro Maljinn, bent on revenge, falls under the eye of the wandering magus Yulwei, who senses something unnatural in her and turns her northward. In the North, the Dogman and the last of Logen's old crew search for a way to survive King Bethod's rule.

Logen reaches Adua and finds himself, to his own bewilderment, taken up by Bayaz, who is quietly assembling a strange company for some undisclosed purpose. Jezal, sharpened by hard training and by his feelings for Ardee, presses on toward the Contest even as court politics churn around him. Glokta, having survived the fallout of his investigation, is handed new and more perilous work by Sult. The wizard's claims and demands force the Union's rulers to take him seriously, whether or not they believe a word he says.

By the book's close the disparate threads begin to knot together. Bayaz reveals just enough of his intentions to bind his chosen companions - Logen, Ferro, Jezal, and others - to a long journey he insists is necessary for the Union's survival: a quest to the far edge of the World in search of an ancient power. War with the North looms as Bethod's ambitions press on the Union's borders, and in the South the Gurkish Empire stirs. Old lives are left behind as the company prepares to set out, none of them fully trusting the old man leading them, and none able to see where he truly means to take them.
